name: Malik Willis
headline: Backup with electric splits now MIA's QB1 wildcard
body:
Willis has been remarkably efficient in tiny spot-duty samples. In 2025 across 4 games he completed 85.7 percent of 35 passes for 422 yards and 3 TDs with zero picks, posting a sparkling 0.486 EPA per play, a 22.0 CPOE and a 91.4 on-target rate while adding 123 rush yards and 2 scores on 22 carries (51.2 fantasy points). His 2024 Green Bay cameo was similar: 6 games, 74.1 percent completions, 3 TDs, 0 INTs, 0.246 EPA per play. The dual-threat profile is real, with rushing usage that props up his floor.

The catch is the leap from clean-pocket relief work to a full starting load; that efficiency will regress hard against starter-level defenses and tougher game scripts. But Miami's depth chart now lists him as QB1, which turns a career clipboard option into a streaming target with a rushing-driven ceiling. If the job holds through camp, he is a late-round dart and bye-week streamer; standalone QB1 value hinges entirely on volume he has never carried.
